channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: These free ChatGPT apps will analyze data, copyedit text, and improve productivity
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. To use ChatGPT more creatively, you can now pick from thousands of free apps called “GPTs.” Each AI app has a special super power.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Software stocks may be primed for a big Wall Street comeback
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Technology Letter and is republished here with permission. It’s been a rough year for software stocks, and a very good year for hardware names such as Nvidia, which just hit a new all-time high, briefly, last week. Maybe it’s time to switch.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: MWC Shanghai 2024: Huawei adopts commercial 5G-A for the mobile AI era
item.description: In 2024, major operators in Asia and the Middle East have already begun deploying 5G-A networks, and some have launched differentiated experience packages based on this technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2's planned public beta has been canceled - 'it would take the development teams away from preparing for the full launch'
item.description: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2 has its planned public beta canceled so Saber Interactive can 'ensure the best possible experience at release'
item.dc:creator: rob.dwiar@futurenet.com (Rob Dwiar) , TechRadar

item.title: Google Chrome to Discontinue Support for Entrust Certificates, Potential Security Improvements Loom
item.description: As of November 1, Google Chrome will no longer trust websites using Entrust certificates by default, potentially paving the way for enhanced security and privacy features.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Hoyoverse's Latest Collaboration Unveils Exclusive Gaming PC Inspired by Intergalactic Gamer
item.description: Hoyoverse, the creators of Honkai: Star Rail, partner with Hyte and iBuyPower to bring a unique gaming PC featuring a branded case and prebuilt system inspired by their popular intergalactic gamer
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood) , TechRadar

item.title: European Union accuses Facebook owner Meta of breaking digital rules with paid ad-free option
item.description: European Union regulators have accused social media company Meta Platforms of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Provider or partner? IT leaders rethink vendor relationships for value
item.description: Shane McDaniel was nearly a year into a modernization effort when he and his IT team for the City of Seguin, Texas, cut over from the city’s legacy network to the upgraded version. On hand for much of the work that Saturday in June 2019 was the rep from Extreme Networks, a Morrisville, N.C.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Autonomous trash-gobbling robot boat wages war on waterway waste
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides the details on the Class 3 Clearbot: an autonomous vessel for efficient marine trash/plant removal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The top 15 supply chain management certifications
item.description: What is supply chain management Supply chain management ( SCM) is the overall process of procuring raw materials to develop into finished products that can be delivered to end-users, such as software, hardware, and other IT and tech services offered by companies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cities most targeted for travel booking scams
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson takes a look at the five travel destinations most frequently targeted by cybercriminals through online booking scams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abandoning doggy doo? DNA will sniff you out and expose you
item.description: Some apartment complexes and homeowners associations are requiring residents to submit DNA samples from their dogs to track who is responsible for piles of dog waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Will China try to make a land grab on the moon?
item.description: “A lot of their so-called civilian space program is a military program. And I think, in effect, we are in a race,” says NASA Administrator Bill Nelson.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: TSA launches 3D scanners to detect explosives
item.description: The new technology will give agents a 3D image that can be turned 360 degrees for enhanced threat protection.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: Opinion: Big tech companies are burning through reservoirs of trust with AI
item.description: Is generative AI a useful productivity tool or a series of overhyped, intrusive products that consume vital resources?
item.dc:creator: Irina Raicu, The Mercury News

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon is reviewing whether Perplexity AI improperly scraped online content
item.description: Amazon is reviewing claims that the artificial intelligence startup Perplexity AI is scraping content — including from prominent news sites — without approval.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: Tech exec’s Pescadero mansion listed for $8 million
item.description: Features of the 6,000-square-foot mansion include a two-story solarium and an indoor swimming pool with underwater channel to the outdoor lap pool.
item.dc:creator: Pueng Vongs, The Mercury News

item.title: With new AI tools available, State Department encourages experimentation
item.description: State wants employees to try out new AI tools like State Chat and North Star, but also share their own use cases to help drive the agency's AI approach. The post With new AI tools available, State Department encourages experimentation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: White House to hold conference for digital creators
item.description: The Biden administration will host digital creators at the White House for the first-ever Creator Economy Conference in August, the director of digital strategy announced Friday. The conference, which will be held Aug.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: DoD’s Fulcrum to serve as north star for its IT objectives
item.description: “I want [Fulcrum] to get all the way down to the individual program managers and have them adjust their day-to-day decisions," said Leslie Beavers. The post DoD’s Fulcrum to serve as north star for its IT objectives first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Google opens access to 2 million context window of Gemini 1.5 Pro
item.description: Google has opened access to the 2-million-token context window of the Gemini 1.5 Pro AI model. The company also is giving developers access to code execution capabilities in the Gemini API and making the Gemma 2 model available in Google AI Studio. These announcements were made on June 27.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Uber, Lyft agree to minimum pay, benefits in Massachusetts settlement
item.description: Uber and Lyft have agreed to offer a minimum wage, paid sick leave and other benefits for drivers as part of a $175 million settlement resolving a multi-year legal battle with Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: EU Vice President Criticizes Apple's Delay in Implementing AI, Calls it Anticompetitive Move
item.description: European Commission Vice President Margrethe Vestager has slammed Apple's decision to delay the introduction of its Apple Intelligence AI, calling it a 'stunning declaration' of anticompetitive behavior.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Enterprise buyer’s guide: Android smartphones for business
item.description: Android dominates smartphone usage throughout the world — in every region except North America and Oceania. Thus, businesses in many regions are likely to support and issue Android devices to employees as their mainstay mobile devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Box Unveils Enhanced Box AI with GPT-4o Integration, Advanced File Support
item.description: Box announces an upgraded version of its artificial intelligence platform, Box AI, featuring seamless integration with GPT-4o, plus enhanced support for image and spreadsheet files and API access to metadata.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: California Bill Seeks to Empower Doxxing Victims with Right to Sue for Up to $30,000
item.description: A proposed California bill aims to provide doxxing victims the right to sue perpetrators in civil court for up to $30,000 in damages, plus attorneys' fees and court costs, in an effort to combat online harassment against LGBTQ individuals and others.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: AI success: Real or hallucination?
item.description: Ever consider how much of AI is a hallucination? Not how much AI output is hallucination, but rather how much of what we hear or read about it is genuine? Could it be that AI success is mostly hype, and that all the hype may be hurting the technology rather than helping? Enterprises need to get to t
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Why NASA astronauts are delayed at the space station after Boeing Starliner launch
item.description: Two astronauts are spending extra time at the International Space Station as NASA and Boeing troubleshoot capsule problems that popped up on the way there.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pure Storage adds AI features for security and performance
item.description: Pure Storage is upping its AI game with a pair of announcements, including a new storage-as-a-service offering that’s designed for AI workloads and new AI-driven security features to detect and prevent ransomware.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A Vatican Radio territory in north Rome once blamed for electro-smog will become a solar farm
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis decreed Wednesday that an area of northern Rome, long the source of controversy because of electromagnetic waves emitted by Vatican Radio towers there, will now house a field of solar panels to fuel Vatican City. Citing the Vatican’s pledge in U.N.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Philanthropy Trends in Silicon Valley: Insights from CEO Nicole Taylor
item.description: As president and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation, Nicole Taylor offers a unique perspective on philanthropic trends shaping the region.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI image-generator startup Stability AI looks for reshoot with new CEO and investor group
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The troubled artificial intelligence company behind image-generator Stable Diffusion is looking for a reshoot with a new CEO and a surge of investment from a group that includes Sean Parker, Facebook’s former president and Napster's co-founder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia won't pay an $8 million ransom after a cyberattack compromised the national data center
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesia's national data center has been compromised by a hacking group asking for a $8 million ransom that the government says it won't pay.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Car dealerships in North America revert to pens and paper after cyberattacks on software provider
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Car dealerships in North America are still wrestling with major disruptions that started last week with cyberattacks on a company whose software is used widely in the auto retail sales sector. CDK Global, a company that provides software for thousands of auto dealers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UN launches global principles to combat online hate and demands big tech take action now
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations chief on Monday launched global principles to combat online hate and lies and demanded that big tech companies use their power to reduce the harm they are doing to people and societies around the world.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
